selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content: You must return JSON only. ALL fields in the schema must be present, even if null or empty.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a cleaner in Los Angeles, I've noticed that social media has become a breeding ground for fake content. It's overwhelming, to say the least. I try to limit my time on social media to just a few times a week, as I've found that it's better for my mental health. When I do go on, I make sure to verify the credibility of the sources I'm reading. I've also started to rely on trusted news outlets and websites that I know are reputable. It's a bit more time-consuming, but it's worth it to avoid getting caught up in fake information. I've even talked to my friends about this issue, and we've started to fact-check each other's posts before sharing them. It's a small step, but it's a start. I wish more people would take the time to think critically about what they're consuming online. We're not just passive consumers; we have the power to create change by being mindful of the information we share and consume. That's how we can make a difference, one post at a time. I've seen it work in my own community, and I believe it can work on a larger scale. We just need to be more aware and more thoughtful in our online interactions. That's the key to managing and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I realized that fake content on social media was a problem when I started to notice how easily misinformation can spread. I didn't want to contribute to the problem, so I took steps to limit my time on social media and to verify the credibility of the sources I consume. I also talked to my friends about the issue and we started to fact-check each other's posts. I believe that if more people took the time to think critically about what they're consuming online, we could create real change. It's not just about not spreading fake information, it's about being mindful of the information we share and consume. I've seen it work in my own community and I believe it can work on a larger scale if we just take the time to be more aware and thoughtful online.
citations: news outlets and websites that I know are reputable
